name: idempotency-key-injection-flips-retry
description: contracts/adapter-pack.md 'Idempotency-key injection': a target configuring idempotency.header does not change the core's judgment by itself — injection happens in the adapter, which flips Request.idempotent to true before the call ever reaches the core. This is the mirror of scenario 04 (non-idempotent-not-retried): the SAME POST, to a target that additionally carries idempotency.header, submitted with idempotent: true (as an adapter would after injecting a key) retries through a 5xx to success instead of terminating with KEEL-E014. The resolved idempotency.header config itself is inert at this layer (contracts/core_api.rs carries no key material) — pinning that the policy parses and resolves without perturbing Tier 1 behavior.
